extraction.one_line_summary: HERMANS Ben defended red jersey by attacking on Green Mountain, holding off late-charging ARU by 3 seconds
tactical_patterns: BMC's Hermans waited through teammates' repeated probing attacks (Morton, Laala) before launching decisive move himself on Green Mountain.
White jersey Kudus bridged early but lacked finishing power, revealing young riders attack too soon on summit finishes.
Astana's Aru closed fastest in final meters, suggesting team misjudged when to commit rather than lacking form.

rider_signals.HERMANS Ben: positive: converted time trial leader strength into climbing win, managing own effort while teammates probed
rider_signals.MORTON Lachlan: neutral: attacking twice for leader suggests BMC used climbing domestiques as tactical decoys
rider_signals.KUDUS Merhawi: negative: white jersey went too early, faded when Hermans countered showing immature summit finish timing
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: positive: fastest late acceleration suggests Astana miscalculated launch point not rider form
